title: Retrieval Review: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders
summary: Retrieval Review: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders retrieves prior skills in an original changed public-service visual layer scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css list styling, css link states, css backgrounds borders in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed public-service visual layer artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey artifact and connect styles.css. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Build the survey design system here. */

steps.title: 01 · predict 1: survey verification checklist redesign · review
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim survives the changed case? Scenario: changed public-service visual layer.
steps.why: Retrieval Review: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in changed public-service visual layer.

content.title: changed public-service visual layer evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Retrieval Review: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Retrieval Review: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Retrieval Review: Lists, Links, Backgrounds, and Borders checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Changed public-service visual layer case 1: removing bullets always removes list semantics from every browser and screen reader.
Changed public-service visual layer case 1: preserve list markup, make grouping visually clear, and verify semantics plus marker alignment after styling.
Changed public-service visual layer case 1: replace every list item with a paragraph and a manually typed bullet character.

options.text: Changed public-service visual layer case 3: a background image communicates essential content as reliably as an img element.
